title: NOC 32201 – Massage Therapists | GTR Immigration
description: Massage Therapists under NOC 32201 are regulated natural health professionals providing therapeutic massage and soft tissue treatment services across Canada. With increasing demand for rehabilitation, chronic pain management, and holistic healthcare services, this TEER 2 occupation continues to offer strong employment opportunities nationwide. For internationally trained professionals, NOC 32201 may qualify under Express Entry and selected Provincial Nominee Programs, supporting a pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group322 – Practitioners of natural healingMinor Group3220 – Practitioners of natural healingNOC Code32201

duties: Massage Therapists perform some or all of the following duties:
Assess clients using range-of-motion and muscle tests
Administer massage therapy techniques including:Soft tissue manipulationRelaxation therapyHydrotherapyTrigger point therapyJoint mobilizationRemedial exercise programs
Provide postural correction, stretching, strengthening, and rehabilitation guidance
Collaborate with physiotherapists, chiropractors, physicians, and psychologists when necessary
employment_requirements: Completion of an 18- to 24-month or 18- to 36-month massage therapy program from an accredited institution
Registration with a regulatory body is mandatory in:
College of Massage Therapists of Ontario
College of Complementary Health Professionals of British Columbia
Internationally educated applicants must complete credential assessment and licensing examinations where required.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $32,800 to $105,900, particularly for experienced therapists operating successful private practices.
job_outlook: Growth in rehabilitation and sports medicine
Rising demand for non-invasive treatment methods
Self-employed and registered practitioners in urban centres may experience stronger income potential.
labour_market_demand: This positive outlook reflects growing awareness of physical rehabilitation, sports therapy, and non-pharmaceutical pain management. Demand is particularly notable in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Manitoba, and Nova Scotia.
Why Immigrate to Canada as a Massage Therapist?
Opportunities for private practice and self-employment
Registered professionals often enjoy strong client demand and flexible work arrangements.
pathways: If you are a qualified Massage Therapist, NOC 32201 may provide a pathway toward permanent residence in Canada.
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your structured pathway toward permanent residence in Canada under NOC 32201.
